OPEN.name: OPEN_after_collision_algebra

degree_bound.name: mu_E5_collision_degree_bound
proof: Agreeing monic leading 1 and the next w high monic coefficients gives deg(diff) ≤ j−w−1.
Additionally agreeing a_{w+1} kills the X^{j-w-1} coefficient of the difference, hence deg(diff) ≤ j−w−2.
degree_bound.statement: If monic degree-j locators Λ_S, Λ_T satisfy Phi_w(S)=Phi_w(T) and a_{w+1}(Λ_S)=a_{w+1}(Λ_T) (coefficient of X^{j-w-1}), then deg(Λ_S − Λ_T) ≤ j − w − 2.

diff_cores.name: R_sing_mu_E5_collision_distinct_cores
proof: Thus any μ_E5 collision is between distinct can-cores — not a within-pencil ambiguity (already removed by A_SP).
diff_cores.statement: On R_sing(z) (singleton top-seam pencils / A_SP residual), if S ≠ T and μ_E5(S)=μ_E5(T), then C_can(S) ≠ C_can(T).

share_min.name: mu_E5_collision_shares_min_root
proof: By definition min_exp(S)=min_exp(T)=m0 means m0 ∈ S and m0 ∈ T.
share_min.statement: If μ_E5(S)=μ_E5(T)=(m0, a) with m0 a domain exponent in S and T (as in the mark definition min_exp), then the corresponding domain value α lies in S ∩ T, so |S ∩ T| ≥ 1 and (X−α) divides both locators.

tool_routing.name: cas_tool_routing_for_open_problems
tool_routing.statement:
Open problem routing to local CAS (this machine):
  P_multi / |A_SP| bounds:
    - Sage/PARI: larger dyadic enumerations (n=32,64) for scaling
    - Wolfram: asymptotics / generating functions for CS pencil counts
    - Second-moment identities already in grande_finale (exact L2)
  Injective residual marks / collision emptiness:
    - Sage/Singular/M2/msolve: polynomial systems for mark collisions
      (two monics, shared high coeffs, shared mark constraints)
    - Sage: multiplicative coset arithmetic on mu_n
    - PARI: fast finite-field poly gcd for collision audits
  Not for this wall: raw Gröbner at deployed (j,w) scale (infeasible).
Use CAS to find structure and kill candidate marks; finite deployed proof must be human-checkable identities + first-match payment.

title: CAS tool routing + mu_E5 collision algebra
